TextENGNew: Sure enough, the men trimming the hedges with brush cutters looked as though they were stationed there to keep me from leaving school grounds.
After all, there was always the chance I might catch on and try to flee.
changeReason: Old 'I tried to escape' (past action performed) vs new 'there was always the chance I might catch on and flee' (hypothetical possibility); JPN describes a possibility, not an actual attempt.

TextENGNew: ...But they must have assumed that wouldn't happen until after school.@k They wouldn't have imagined I'd realize it now and bolt right this instant.
Walking out through the front gate was out of the question—they'd spot me immediately.
I circled around to the back, confirmed that no one was near the curry garden, dashed across it, and plunged into the woods.
Snapping dry branches underfoot, clambering over moss-covered fallen trees, ducking beneath irritating tangles of brush.
changeReason: New adds specific details from JPN: 苔むした (moss-covered) and 不愉快な枝葉 (irritating branches) absent from old translation.

TextENGNew: Before long, I emerged onto a narrow path.
It was fortunate that over the past year, through club activities and the like, I'd become intimately familiar with the village's various back roads and shortcuts.
Now then... what do I do...?
The girl I'd asked to pass on my message must have already told Chie-sensei about my early departure.
By now, they must have learned I'd escaped and be in a panic.
``Did anyone else speak with Ryuugu-san before she left?``
Everyone in the class looked at one another and shook their heads.
Chie-sensei seemed somewhat displeased that Rena had left without speaking to her directly, illness or not.
``But Rena-san has been looking unwell these past few days.``
``Rena even left her bag behind.@k@vS09/03/170300434. ...What's going on with her?``
``...I have a really bad feeling about this.``
I was probably the only one who had some idea of what was going on.
changeReason: Old 'I think I know' (general) vs new 'I was probably the only one who had some idea'; JPN 俺にだけは = 'only I' — the exclusivity is significant.

TextENGNew: ...I had no proof, but I had a terrible premonition.
Had she taken some other absurd story at face value again... and gone off to do something reckless...?
I hurried home.
Dad was either already at his new job or out shopping.@k ...In any case, the house was empty.
With them already on the move, my home was no longer a safe haven.
These were people who had trained with the intention of storming the Ministry of Construction, people capable of actually kidnapping a government minister's grandson.
Locking a few doors wouldn't mean a thing.@k If anything, it would be like voluntarily cornering myself in a dead end.
changeReason: Old metaphor 'hiding in a box they could open' vs new 'cornering myself in a dead end'; JPN 袋小路 = dead end, a different spatial metaphor.

TextENGNew: ...I realized I could no longer stay in my own home.
It was a terribly, terribly sad and frustrating thing.
I'd killed Rina and Teppei so that my father and I could reclaim our peaceful life.
And yet—how had it come to this?!
Something Keiichi-kun once said resurfaced in my mind, expressing my feelings better than I ever could.
``Damn Takano-san, what a parting gift she left us...!``
changeReason: New adds 'Damn' conveying the curse particle め in JPN 鷹野さんめ, expressing frustration absent from old translation.

TextENGNew: Why had she given me those terrifying scrapbooks?!
Why had she burdened me with the secret of that unimaginable conspiracy?!
I was just an innocent bystander who got dragged into it.
changeReason: Old 'She got me into this' (blaming Takano) vs new 'I was just an innocent bystander who got dragged in' (self-identification as victim); different framing.

TextENGNew: I'm not at fault—it's all Miyo-san's doing!
But it was me who showed interest in her stories, who let curiosity drive me to hear more.
It was me who let those Ibaraki memories—the ones I'd worked so hard to forget—resurface, and then told Miyo-san about them.
It was me who leaned on Miyo-san, hoping that confiding in her might bring some kind of salvation.
I should have left those bizarre memories buried and forgotten!
If I had, I would never have been pulled into this monstrous conspiracy, and right now I'd be having fun with everyone at club activities, just like any other day!!
...But even if I hadn't known, the conspiracy would still have been advancing beneath the surface.
And when 'the Day of Oyashiro-sama's Revival' came, I would simply have been swallowed up as one anonymous figure in a panicking mob, oblivious to the truth.
If the world was going to be destroyed regardless, would I have been happier meeting that day in ignorance?
Or happier struggling with knowledge, only to be consumed anyway?
...If the destination is the same either way, if both choices lead to the same outcome... I'll fight.@k I choose the option that lets me fight.
That's how I seized my happiness before.
...When Rina wormed her way into my father's life and began eroding our home,@k I felt as though it were the end of the world. Every day I lived on the verge of being crushed by despair.
And I thought no greater misfortune could possibly exist.
But looking back on it now, that was nothing particularly terrible.
My enemies were exactly two: Rina Mamiya and Teppei Houjou.
I knew who the enemies were, I knew how many there were—all I had to do was lure them out one at a time and ambush them. A ridiculously easy problem.
What about now?
I don't even know who the enemy is, and I can't begin to estimate their numbers or scale.
The people I've always believed to be allies—how far can I really trust them, and how many might actually be enemies?@k I can't even tell that!
This is on a completely different level from last time.
A despair that truly feels like the end of the world.
This time, it genuinely felt as though no greater misfortune could exist.
But... is that really true...?
The trouble with Rina felt like the end of the world at the time, but looking back now, it wasn't that bad.
Maybe one day I'll look back on this crisis and laugh at how small it actually was.@k ...No—that day will come.@k It absolutely will!
So I must not think this is the end of the world.
Fight.@k I can fight.@k I can still struggle!
I'll eat grass and drink mud if I have to.
Even if it seems futile, I'll bet on that one percent chance of victory.
If Miyo-san hadn't entrusted those scrapbooks to me, I wouldn't even have a seat at the table to make that bet.
So receiving those scrapbooks was indeed a stroke of fortune, a chance, an option that could let me seize my future.
If I don't fight, they'll unleash that horrifying parasite and the village will face catastrophe.
According to legend, when the 'demons' first emerged from Onigafuchi Swamp, the infected became 'demons' instantly.
And that was despite being inside the village.
The notion that symptoms only appear when you leave the village is merely something that applies to the weakened parasites of later generations.
If the parasites possessed their original, primordial power, they could recreate 'the curse of Oyashiro-sama' even within the village itself!
That would be nothing less than replaying the legend of Oyashiro-sama from the beginning.
Humans and demons would divide, a terrifying slaughter would erupt, and... in the midst of it all, 'Oyashiro-sama' would descend and subdue the chaos.
That is exactly what they're after!!
The people would prostrate themselves before that miracle, and only those who embraced the faith would be saved.
...I see it now... I understand their true aim...
They intend to reenact the legend of Oyashiro-sama from scratch—to stage 'the Second Coming of Oyashiro-sama'!
That's right—isn't Rika-chan said to be the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama?@k The stage is already set!
They'll use the 'parasite' as the 'curse.'
Then... how will they stage 'Oyashiro-sama's subjugation'?
Of course!@k There must be a medicine—a cure!!
At the same time they created parasites with their original primordial power, they must have also created a drug to treat them.
They'll save only the believers with that cure, weeding out the village's population until only devout followers remain.
And then, in a Hinamizawa populated entirely by believers, they'll reign as absolute rulers!
No, wait... isn't it naive to assume this horrifying act of bioterrorism would be confined to Hinamizawa alone...?
History has proven again and again that those who seek to dominate through faith inevitably crave to spread that faith ever wider.
In that case, during their research into the parasite, wouldn't they also have worked to overcome its weakness—the fact that it can only thrive in the limited area of Hinamizawa?
According to Miyo-san's research, the parasites grow increasingly hostile the farther their hosts travel from Hinamizawa.
In other words, distance is a factor.
Extending the range of habitable environment.@k Expanding the radius of the territory where the parasites can thrive.
How far has their research progressed?
From Hinamizawa to Okinomiya?@k Surely not the entire city?
...No—they might have covered the entire prefecture!
It could spread to neighboring prefectures, engulfing an entire region.
This was no longer something that could be called a mere scheme.
It was a grand and terrifying conspiracy worthy of the word 'coup d'état'!!
And X-Day for this conspiracy was probably not far off.
I could tell from the way they'd murdered Miyo-san and from the brazenness of the pressure being applied to me.
With X-Day approaching, they were no longer choosy about how they eliminated even the slightest sources of doubt!
I was the only one who could stand against this terrifying conspiracy.
The only person I could trust now was myself.@k ...And perhaps Keiichi-kun.@k But no one else!!
Mii-chan was absolutely out of the question.@k Rika-chan was no good either.@k And Satoko-chan, inseparable from Rika-chan, was no good too.@k No adults, nobody—none of them could be trusted!!
I had no allies at all.
The only thing closing in was the enemy's sinister reach.
The ticking of the clock counting down to the end did nothing but drive me to frantic impatience.
How should I fight?@k What should I do?!
First, I needed time to think.
And for that, I needed a 'safe zone' where I could focus without distraction.
Fortunately, I already happened to have such a 'safe zone' from the very start.
My hideout at the garbage dump!
No one knew about it. Absolutely safe.
Even if, by some chance, they closed in on it, I knew every escape route and hiding spot in the surrounding area.
But that hideout had one major weakness.
A very simple one.@k I had no food there.
That's why I'd come home first.
I opened my father's drawer and unlocked his cash box again.
The contents appeared largely unchanged from the last time I'd looked inside.
There were still piles of crisp ten-thousand-yen bills.
I took every last one.@k I hadn't counted, but there must have been around eighty of them.
This should be... more than enough.
If I could evade them long enough to spend all this money... that in itself would be a tremendous feat.
Besides, I might need to bribe someone along the way.@k Having extra cash could never be a disadvantage.
I didn't take the bank passbook.
If they had the bank staked out, that would be the end.@k The risk of trying to withdraw was incalculable.
Then my backpack.@k The key to my bicycle.
I gathered everything I thought might be useful and tossed it all into the backpack.
A little extra weight was fine.@k I could sort through it at the hideout and leave anything unnecessary behind.
I checked the clock.
Nearly thirty minutes had passed since I'd arrived home.
I'd stayed too long!
I rushed out of the house.
Then I stopped at an out-of-the-way general store and bought up all the sweet rolls and canned hardtack I could fit in my backpack.
The old woman minding the shop was fortunately too feeble-minded to show any interest in such an excessive purchase.
...Even so, once the owner checked the register that night, the abnormal sale would come to light.
...That didn't matter. I wouldn't be walking openly through the village in daylight again anyway.
My backpack bulged, and the cans jabbed painfully into my back with every step.@k But none of that mattered.
Still shouldering that heavy load, I made it safely to my hideout.
Then I organized my supplies.
...The weight had been worth it—I'd secured a considerable amount of food.
For water, I could use the tap at the nearby abandoned construction office.
The lack of vegetables nagged at me slightly, but this was no time for luxuries.
Once I'd finished organizing, I retrieved the scrapbooks I'd hidden here and opened them, setting my mind racing at full speed.
All right, what now?@k How do I stand against this enormous, terrifying conspiracy?
Don't panic. Stay calm, but act swiftly!
There might be almost no time left—so I had to hurry, but what should I actually do?!
How carefree it would be to simply run to school on an overslept morning.
How easy, when you know exactly where you need to go!
Right now, I didn't even know where to head or what to accomplish!
At this point, I should tell Ooishi-san everything.
...The odds that he's an enemy were high.
But if so, so be it—it would simply become a declaration of war!
But if he would stand with me... that was my sole, slender thread of hope.
To stand alone against a conspiracy of such terrifying scale—one that would surely throw all of Japan into chaos—I was far too fragile!
I'd have to use a public phone to make the call.
...In this village, there was only one public phone that could be used without being easily seen.@k That phone booth!
It sat in a desolate spot, all by itself. At night, bugs swarmed around its light—not the most pleasant place.@k But I couldn't think of any other way to contact Ooishi-san.
Still, even though it was in a secluded spot, using it in daylight was too dangerous.
...I would wait for nightfall and make the call under cover of darkness.
I'd heard that Ooishi-san had been holed up at the station ever since Miyo-san's case, so fortunately, I should be able to reach him no matter what time I called.@k I even had his direct work number.
But the sky was still bright.
Time was running out, yet I had to wait for darkness—what a maddening contradiction!
Impatient, I cursed the lingering, slow-setting sun.
Then let me find something I can do while I wait for it to get dark.
Don't waste a single moment—what can I do, what can I do, what can I do!!
...After thinking it through, there was one nagging worry I wanted to put to rest.
I was stuck here until nightfall regardless.@k Sitting still and doing nothing would only wear me out with anxiety.
With that decision made, I left the hideout.
In front of the Ryuugu household gate, Rena's father was pacing restlessly back and forth.
Then came the tinkle of a bicycle bell approaching.
``Officer!!@k@vS09/00/172900071. Over here, over here!!``
The person who'd arrived on the bicycle was the local Hinamizawa police officer.
He'd apparently ridden hard to get there—the middle-aged officer was breathing heavily.
``Haah, haah!@k@vS09/00/chu17002. Sorry to keep you waiting. Please tell me what happened.``
``I know for certain I locked up when I left.@k@vS09/00/172900073. But when I got back, the front door was wide open...``
The father led the officer inside.
``And then... in my room... my cash box had been cleaned out...``
``Sir, is this exactly how you found it?@k@vS09/00/chu17004. You haven't touched anything?``
``No, I didn't touch the cash box because I figured the burglar might have left fingerprints.@k@vS09/00/172900076. But I did check inside, so I've touched some of the contents...``
``I see, I see. And what was taken?``
``The bank passbooks and seal are still there, but... all the cash is gone.``

``The passbooks and seal weren't taken, huh?@k@vS09/00/chu17007. Well, that's a small mercy.@k@vS09/00/chu17008. ...These days, pros only take cash because passbooks leave a trail.@k@vS09/00/chu17009. Are you sure you locked up properly?``
``...Opening the cash box on the spot instead of taking it with him—this guy knows what he's doing.@k@vS09/00/chu17011. So, roughly how much money was taken?``
``...Hmm, I hadn't counted it recently, but... I believe there were close to a hundred ten-thousand-yen bills.``
``Oh... I see...@k@vS09/00/chu17013. Did you tell anyone there was that much cash in here?``
``...No, I didn't tell anyone. But... those women might have known there was money in this house...``
``Those women?@k@vS09/00/chu17015. Hmm, could you tell me more about that...?``
``...Well, this is a rather embarrassing story...@k@vS09/00/172900081. There was a woman named Rina Mamiya, and...``
Just then, the phone in the Ryuugu household rang shrilly.
``...Oh, excuse me. Let me get that.@k@vS09/00/172900083. I'll be right back.``
Muttering something about bad timing, the father grabbed the receiver a bit roughly.
``Hello, Ryuugu residence.``
``Oh!@k@vS09/01/170100778. ...Um, sorry to bother you.@k@vS09/01/170100779. This is Maebara. Is Rena-san there?``
``Reina hasn't come home yet.``

``Ah, I'm sorry, but I'm in the middle of something right now.@k@vS09/00/172900087. If this is about getting together to play, could you call back later...?@k@vS09/00/172900088. I'm sorry. Thank you.``
All I could do was stand there in stunned silence.
On the surface, nothing appeared to have changed.
No—in fact, I'd even struggled to find the right spot at all, so thoroughly did it blend in.
It was only because a nearby tree had a distinctive knot that I could identify the location.
That was how seamlessly the place where I'd buried Rina and the others had been concealed—as if nothing had ever happened.
That's precisely why I'd initially thought I must have come to the wrong spot.
But driving the shovel into the ground would tell me the truth.
You can easily feel whether the earth has been dug before.
So, after some trial and error, I managed to pinpoint the exact place where I'd buried the body bags.
...But the real problem was... the body bags weren't there!
Why?@k Why are they gone?!
Did I bury them deeper than this?
No, that wasn't possible.
I could feel the resistance of the shovel in undisturbed earth.@k I hadn't buried anything deeper than this!
...This was completely unexpected.
Coming here to check on the bodies had been... nothing more than killing time, really.
I hadn't wanted to sit idle in my hideout until dark, so I'd come to verify that the buried corpses were still in place.
Confirm they were peacefully interred, let out a sigh of relief.@k That was supposed to be all there was to it!!
Blindsided by this development, I felt cold sweat plastering itself across my entire back.
What does this mean?@k What does this mean?!
I didn't even need to think calmly to understand.
Corpses don't come back to life and crawl out of the ground.
Then there was only one possibility—someone had dug them up!
Who, and for what purpose?!
Could some chance witness have been there, dug up the bodies after we left, and reported them to the police...?
No, that was impossible.
It had been quite some time since we buried them.@k We'd done it before Watanagashi, in fact.
So if the police had found them, Ooishi would already have known about Rina's and Teppei's deaths when he contacted me.
But Ooishi hadn't said a word about it.@k There was no reason for him to have hidden it deliberately.
The answer was clear: the police still didn't know about Rina and the others.
Then why were the bodies gone?
Only one answer remained.
...Someone who knew they were buried here.
Someone who'd helped bury them had come back afterward, dug out every last body, and taken them away!!
The first person who came to mind was Mion—no, the Sonozaki family—but I couldn't see a motive.
The murders of Rina and the others had nothing to do with the Sonozaki family.
They wouldn't want to be saddled with some false accusation.@k They'd want to distance themselves from it as much as possible.
So why would they deliberately take possession of those corpses—the equivalent of the joker in Old Maid...?!
...If I were Mii-chan, what would I be thinking?@k If I were the club president, what would I do...?
The joker may be the card everyone fears in Old Maid, but change the game and it can become the most powerful trump card of all.
...What if the joker became a devastating trump card...?
Something electric shot up my spine.@k ...And at the same time, I realized—they were one step ahead...!
The Sonozaki family had recovered the bodies to use them as a joker—a trump card to sell me out to the police!
The Sonozaki family surely wanted to eliminate me through their assassination squad, but they'd taken out insurance in case that failed.
What they feared most was me joining forces with the police.
If they could preemptively sever that possibility, I would be left with no allies at all.
And then I'd be a fish on a cutting board—unable to run, unable to resist!
In other words, they intended to use those corpses to... frame me for the murders of Rina and Teppei and turn the police against me!
If the police put out a warrant for me as a murder suspect, they would never, ever listen to what I had to say.
...Even the last people who might have been my allies would join the ranks of those hunting me!
But I was sure they wouldn't want to play this joker lightly.
After all, it was a fact that Mion Sonozaki had helped bury those bodies.
And with some conspiracy's X-Day approaching, they couldn't want police prowling all over Hinamizawa.
...Tonight, I would call Ooishi, confess everything, and ask for help.
...I would tell him the absolute truth—things I hadn't even told Keiichi-kun.
Just getting Ooishi to believe me was already a long-shot gamble... and on top of that, the enemy held the joker of those body bags...!
...Impressive, Mii-chan. Exactly what I'd expect from the club president.
...What was a life-or-death battle for me was, to Mion, nothing more than a move in the game being played on the board called Hinamizawa.
I let out a faint grin at the word 'game.'
...Even in our regular club activities, I fought for my life because I dreaded the penalty games.
Now that had become literally true.
There could only be one reason the enemy was going to such lengths to corner me.
It was because... they feared me.
I'd assumed I was nothing but a powerless girl flailing about in confusion.
But apparently, the enemy didn't see it that way.
If they truly believed I was harmless, they wouldn't take such risks to hound me.
Which meant... the scrapbooks Miyo-san had entrusted to me must be far more devastating than I'd realized.
...Well, well.@k They're not the only ones with a joker.@k I've got one too.
That joker is so powerful, so deadly, that it could shatter their entire scheme to pieces.@k ...That's exactly why they're so desperate to find me!
Ah ha ha...
This must be what it feels like when a cornered mouse bites the cat.@k Suddenly, I felt my fear of the enemy begin to fade.
Just as I feared them, they feared me.
...So this game was perfectly even.@k My position wasn't nearly as disadvantageous as I'd thought!
Coming here had been the right call.
I'd learned what card the enemy had acquired. That advantage was no small thing.
I grinned fiercely, bolstering my own trembling resolve with that smile.
There was no need to stay here any longer.
For the enemy, this was yet another place connected to me.
...Lingering here was not necessarily safe.
I decided to return to my hideout.
On the way back, my path took me near my house.
And I immediately noticed something was wrong.
I hid behind cover and observed the front of my house...
...The person speaking with my father was the local police officer.
...I strained my ears to catch their conversation.
``Yes.@k@vS09/00/172900090. It's usually parked right there...``
My father was pointing to the spot where I normally parked my bicycle.
``Hmmm...@k@vS09/00/chu17017. Understood.@k@vS09/00/chu17018. Please contact us when your daughter comes home.@k@vS09/00/chu17019. We'll keep looking into this as well.``
...Impressive, Mii-chan.@k Already played the joker, have you...?
Mion always struck first—seizing the initiative, overwhelming the opposition in one push, and claiming first place.
The police already appeared to be after me.
...My already slim chances of explaining the conspiracy to Ooishi had just plummeted further.
No... that's precisely why it has to be Ooishi.

Because he views the Sonozaki main house as his enemy and is trying to expose everything in this, his final year before retirement,@k he's the one person who might not be ensnared by the Sonozaki family's trap—the one who might become my ally...!
Of course, there was also a very real chance that this had all been a trap from the beginning.
His talk of treating the Sonozaki family as adversaries could be nothing but a lie to gain my trust.
...And yet, the enemy's joker actually gave me a measure of hope.
If Ooishi were one of them, this joker was being played far too early.
Ooishi was not in league with them.
That's precisely why they'd played it now—to drive a wedge between us before I could reach him.
...No, wait—could that be exactly what they want me to think, a trick to make me trust Ooishi...?!
When Mion presents you with two choices, more often than not, you're better off going with your gut rather than agonizing over it.
Mion loves to tangle people up in their own thinking.
...So the moment you start overthinking, you've already fallen into her trap.
I would proceed as planned.@k Wait a little longer, and once full darkness fell, I would call Ooishi.
If he agreed to be my ally, we would discuss the way forward together.
And if he didn't?
...Figuring that out, it seemed, was the task I needed to complete before nightfall.
